data.body:
## Description

**What**: Verify end-to-end that a freshly onboarded project's enforcement infrastructure actually works — hooks fire on first commit and config is parseable.
**Why**: Individual stories may each pass their own criteria but the integrated result could still fail. This story validates the complete onboarding output as a system.
**Scope**:
- IN: Verify git pre-commit hooks (test gate, review gate) execute on first commit attempt; verify generated dso-config.conf is parseable by read-config.sh with no missing required keys; verify ticket system smoke test passed; write an integration test that exercises the full onboarding output
- OUT: Fixing any issues found (those go back to the relevant story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a freshly onboarded project's git pre-commit hooks fire correctly on first commit (test gate and review gate execute)
  ← Satisfies: "End-to-end acceptance"
- When this story is complete, the generated dso-config.conf is parseable by read-config.sh with no missing required keys
  ← Satisfies: "End-to-end acceptance"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] This is an integration-level validation story — may need a fixture project or temp directory to exercise the full onboarding flow
- [Reliability] Test must cover the 3 hook manager variants (Husky, pre-commit, bare .git/hooks/)
